name: geomaster
tagline: Geospatial reference skill: install commands plus 500+ Python code snippets for remote sensing, GIS, spatial ML, and cloud-native geo workfl
verdict: No security red flags found: all code is plain-text, network calls (STAC, Earth Engine, OSM) match the stated geospatial purpose, and the one credential-shaped line (AWSSession) is an unfilled library-usage example, not exfiltration. Dependencies are reputable, well-known geo packages. Quality gaps are minor: no negative-trigger guidance, no explicit output

highlights.summary: No 'do not use for' / negative-trigger guidance anywhere in the frontmatter or body, so an agent has no signal for when geomaster is the wrong tool (e.g. non-geospatial data science tasks).
No explicit statement of what the skill should hand back to the user (file, report, summary format); it reads as a reference library rather than a task with a defined output contract.

Code snippets have essentially no error handling (e.g. classify_imagery lines 220-249, terrain_metrics lines 179-195 assume well-formed inputs); failure guidance is deferred entirely to an unfetched r
